frameworks.name: dissociation of perceptual decision and confidence
frameworks.one_line: confidence evaluation is a process distinct from the perceptual decision itself

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation with premature commitment
frameworks.one_line: observers commit to a choice early while continuing to monitor sensory evidence

frameworks.name: EEG source localization
frameworks.one_line: spectral and source-localized neural signatures index distinct cognitive processes

core_question: Can neural signatures separate the computation of perceptual confidence from the perceptual decision itself?
key_assumptions: perceptual decision and confidence evaluation are separable cognitive processes
motor-cortex spectral power indexes decision commitment
continued evidence monitoring after commitment supports confidence
manipulations: protracted perceptual decision-making task that extends evidence presentation
comparison of neural activity before vs. after decision commitment
key_findings: motor-cortex spectral patterns reveal premature decision commitment
neural representation of perceptual decision evidence attenuates after commitment
distinct neural representation tracks confidence computation
confidence sources localize to superior parietal and orbitofrontal cortices
interpretations: confidence relies on a neural circuit distinct from perceptual decision-making
confidence-related resources are recruited even after the perceptual choice is made
